decisions.title: Auth module shipped with zero P0 bugs.
decisions.detail: Alex confirmed the successful shipment of the auth module last Friday, indicating no critical bugs were found.

evidence.quote: Alex opened by confirming the auth module shipped last Friday with zero P0 bugs.

decisions.title: JWT token expiry set to 24 hours.
decisions.detail: The JWT token expiry is set to 24 hours, as previously agreed in Sprint 2.

decisions.title: Password reset emails are not rate-limited.
decisions.detail: Carol identified a risk that password reset emails currently lack rate-limiting protection.

evidence.quote: Carol flagged that password reset emails are not being rate-limited.

decisions.title: Password reset rate limit is 3 attempts per hour.
decisions.detail: A rate limit of a maximum of 3 password reset attempts per hour was agreed upon in the security review on 2026-06-28.

evidence.quote: Password reset rate limit: 3 per hour

decisions.title: API gateway will use AWS API Gateway.
decisions.detail: The API gateway implementation will utilize AWS API Gateway.

evidence.quote: API gateway: AWS API Gateway, 100 req/min per user cap

decisions.title: API gateway rate limit set to 100 requests per minute per user.
decisions.detail: The API gateway will enforce a rate limit of 100 requests per minute per user, based on Ben's recommendation from load tests.

evidence.quote: API gateway: AWS API Gateway, 100 req/min per user cap

decisions.title: Mobile app team is blocked by auth API spec publication.
decisions.detail: The mobile app team's progress is dependent on and blocked until the authentication API specification is published.

evidence.quote: Alex noted the mobile app team is blocked until the auth API spec is published.

decisions.title: Auth API spec target publish date is 2026-07-05.
decisions.detail: The target date for publishing the auth API specification is July 5, 2026.

decisions.title: Automated integration tests required for auth endpoints.
decisions.detail: Carol requested that all three authentication endpoints (login, refresh, reset) must have automated integration tests implemented before the Sprint 3 sign-off.

evidence.quote: Carol wants all three auth endpoints (login, refresh, reset) to have automated integration tests before Sprint 3 sign-off.

decisions.title: Fallback behavior if API gateway fails.
decisions.detail: A question was raised regarding the system's fallback behavior in the event of an API gateway outage.

evidence.quote: What is the fallback behaviour if the API gateway goes down?

decisions.title: Refresh token invalidation upon password reset.
decisions.detail: A question was posed to determine if refresh tokens are automatically invalidated when a user resets their password.

evidence.quote: Are refresh tokens invalidated on password reset?

implementation_scope: Implement rate limiting for password reset email requests.
Configure API Gateway for rate limiting on a per-user basis.
Develop and publish the Authentication API specification.
Implement automated integration tests for the login, refresh, and reset authentication endpoints.

constraints.text: Auth API specification must be published by 2026-07-05.
Password reset email requests must be rate-limited to a maximum of 3 attempts per hour.
API gateway requests must be rate-limited to 100 requests per minute per user.
Mobile app team is blocked until the Auth API specification is published.

architectural_decisions.text: API gateway implementation will utilize AWS API Gateway.
JWT token expiry is set to 24 hours.

open_technical_questions: What is the fallback behavior if the AWS API Gateway goes down?
Should refresh tokens be invalidated automatically when a user resets their password?

acceptance_criteria: Given a user successfully authenticates and receives a JWT token, When 24 hours have elapsed since the token's issuance, Then attempts to use the token for protected resources must result in an authentication failure.

Given a user initiates password reset requests, When the user submits more than 3 password reset requests within a 60-minute period for the same account, Then all subsequent requests exceeding this limit within that period must be rejected.
Given a user submits 3 or fewer password reset requests within a 60-minute period for the same account, Then all requests must be successfully processed.

Given an authenticated user sends requests to the API gateway, When the user sends more than 100 requests within a 60-second period, Then all subsequent requests exceeding this limit within that period must receive an HTTP 429 (Too Many Requests) response.
Given an authenticated user sends requests to the API gateway, When the user sends 100 or fewer requests within a 60-second period, Then all requests must be processed normally (not rate-limited).
test_scenarios: Verify a valid JWT token is accepted immediately after issuance for a protected resource.
Verify a valid JWT token is accepted at 23 hours and 59 minutes after issuance for a protected resource.
Verify an expired JWT token (e.g., 24 hours and 1 minute after issuance) is rejected for a protected resource.
Attempt 3 password resets for a user within 59 minutes and verify all successful outcomes (e.g., email sent).
Attempt a 4th password reset for the same user within the same 59 minutes and verify it is rejected with an appropriate error message.
Attempt a 4th password reset for the same user after 60 minutes have passed since the first request and verify it is successful.
Attempt password resets for different users within the same hour and verify rate limiting is applied per user, not globally.
Send 100 requests within 59 seconds to an API endpoint and verify all succeed.
Send 101 requests within 59 seconds to an API endpoint and verify the 101st request is rejected with an HTTP 429 response.
Send requests exceeding the API gateway limit, wait 60 seconds from the first request, and verify new requests are processed normally.
edge_cases: Test JWT token validity for tokens issued exactly at the 24-hour mark (boundary condition).
Test concurrent password reset attempts for the same user account.
Test password reset attempts where the email format is invalid but still considered an 'attempt' for rate limiting purposes.
Test API gateway rate limiting behavior for requests arriving precisely at the 60-second interval.
Test a sudden burst of API requests significantly exceeding the API gateway limit.
Test API gateway rate limiting for unauthenticated requests (if any endpoints allow them).
unresolved_behaviors: What is the expected system behavior or fallback if the AWS API Gateway experiences an outage or fails to respond?
Are refresh tokens invalidated automatically when a user successfully resets their password?

decision_history: Auth module shipped last Friday with zero P0 bugs, confirming successful delivery without critical issues.
JWT token expiry is set to 24 hours, as previously agreed in Sprint 2.
A rate limit of 3 password reset attempts per hour was agreed upon in the security review on 2026-06-28.
The API gateway implementation will utilize AWS API Gateway.
The API gateway will enforce a rate limit of 100 requests per minute per user, based on load test recommendations.
All three authentication endpoints (login, refresh, reset) must have automated integration tests implemented before the Sprint 3 sign-off.
dependencies: Mobile app team is blocked until the authentication API specification is published.
risks: Password reset emails are currently not rate-limited, posing a security vulnerability (Likelihood: Confirmed, Impact: High).
Undefined fallback behavior if the AWS API Gateway experiences an outage, potentially leading to system instability or degraded service (Likelihood: Medium, Impact: High).
open_questions: Are refresh tokens automatically invalidated when a user resets their password? (Owned by PM/QA - clarifies a critical security and functional requirement)
milestones: Automated integration tests for all auth endpoints (login, refresh, reset) implemented before Sprint 3 sign-off.

system_requirements: The system SHALL issue JSON Web Tokens (JWT) with an expiry of 24 hours.
The system SHALL rate-limit password reset attempts to a maximum of 3 attempts per hour per user.
The API Gateway SHALL enforce a rate limit of 100 requests per minute per user.
The system's authentication endpoints (login, refresh, reset) SHALL have automated integration tests implemented.
The authentication module SHALL support user login functionality (inferred — needs confirmation).
The authentication module SHALL support JWT refresh functionality (inferred — needs confirmation).
The authentication module SHALL support password reset functionality (inferred — needs confirmation).
data_flows: User authentication requests (login, refresh) flow from client applications to the Authentication module via the API Gateway.
Password reset requests flow from client applications to the Authentication module via the API Gateway.
The Authentication module issues JWT tokens to client applications upon successful authentication or refresh.
The Authentication module initiates the sending of password reset emails to users.
interface_contracts: Auth API contract: Definition for 'login', 'refresh', and 'reset' endpoints, including input payloads, output responses, and error conditions, to be published by 2026-07-05.
API Gateway contract: All API requests passing through the gateway will be subject to a rate limit of 100 requests per minute per user.
Authentication endpoint contract: Issued JWT tokens SHALL contain an 'exp' (expiration time) claim set to 24 hours from the time of issuance.

constraints.text: The authentication API specification MUST be published by 2026-07-05.

architectural_decisions.text: The API gateway component SHALL be implemented using AWS API Gateway.

open_analysis_questions: What is the required fallback behavior of the system in the event of an AWS API Gateway outage?
SHALL refresh tokens be automatically invalidated when a user performs a password reset?

stakeholder.business_summary: The core authentication module has been successfully delivered without critical issues, providing a stable foundation for user access. However, the mobile app development team is currently blocked awaiting the final specifications for the authentication API, with a target publication date of July 5th.
key_impact: **Accelerated Mobile Development**: Publishing the API specification by July 5th is critical to unblock the mobile app team, ensuring timely progress on the overall product.
**Enhanced Security**: Key security measures like API request limits and a password reset attempt limit are being implemented to protect user accounts and system stability.
**Risk Mitigation**: A current vulnerability exists where password reset emails are not yet rate-limited, posing a risk of abuse that needs immediate attention.
**Product Quality**: Automated testing for all login functions will ensure a more reliable and robust user experience.
major_unresolved_questions: What is our plan if the system managing all incoming requests (API gateway) experiences an outage?
For security, are active user sessions automatically invalidated when a user resets their password?

review.reviewer_notes: All role outputs are consistent with the extracted decisions, well-structured, and suitable for their intended audiences. No hallucinations, quality issues, or sensitive content were detected. The SA view correctly identifies inferred functionalities as needing confirmation.
